allFindings.title: 19 fractional-pixel text-[N.Npx] values are invisible to the check:type-scale.mjs ratchet's integer-only regex
allFindings.measured: digest-blended.tsx has 6 fractional sites alone (lines 233, 251, 314, 384, 387, 412, 420, 490 — mix of 10.5px/13.5px/11.5px). Repo-wide: text-[10.5px]×9, text-[11.5px]×6, text-[12.5px]×3, text-[13.5px]×1 = 19 total. scripts/check-type-scale.mjs's pattern `/text-\[(\d+)px\]/g` requires digits immediately followed by `px]`; `10.5px]` never matches, so `npm run check:type-scale` reports zero violations even though none of these values is on the 8/9/10/11/13/15/18/22/24/28/36px scale.
allFindings.expected: Ratchet pattern should match decimal px values too (e.g. `/text-\[(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)px\]/g`) so fractional off-scale sizes are tracked like their integer counterparts.
allFindings.note: Other affected files: PlanSourceSelector.tsx:101, Library.tsx:598, food-search/FoodSearchResultRow.tsx:60, suppr/trajectory-card.tsx:150+165, suppr/portion-picker.tsx:101/125/161/178, suppr/today-barcode-dialog.tsx:532, app/pricing/PaywallTrajectoryChart.tsx:240.

allFindings.title: Heading/label font-weight has no governing rule — 5 distinct weights used interchangeably for structurally-equivalent roles
allFindings.measured: DiscoverFeed.tsx:447 renders the page title 'Discover' at `font-extrabold` (700→800 in Tailwind's scale) — the only page title in the app using extrabold. Repo-wide on serif-headline elements: font-medium×101 / font-semibold×8 / font-normal×5. On uppercase section-labels: font-bold×99 / font-semibold×84 / font-medium×17 / font-extrabold×8.
allFindings.expected: theme.css's own h1/h2/h3 base rules standardize on font-weight-semibold (600) for headlines and .section-label standardizes on 600 for overlines; explicit overrides should not introduce a 5th/6th competing weight without a documented reason.
allFindings.note: This is the weight axis of the same section-label-drift / page-title-drift signatures reported above, broken out per the census's part-(d) ask.

allFindings.title: Profile screen's group-divider headings ('People', 'Settings', 'Creator tools', 'Legal') are sentence-case bold serif — a third, undocumented section-label treatment
allFindings.measured:
`

People

` (line 549; identical pattern repeats at lines 578 'Settings', 744 'Creator tools', 786 'Legal'). No font-family override → inherits Newsreader serif from the global h3 rule. No uppercase, no letter-spacing overline treatment at all — a third convention alongside the uppercase-tracked-caps pattern (findings above) and the canonical `.section-label`.
allFindings.expected: Same structural-label role (dividing a settings-style list into groups) as the uppercase overlines used elsewhere (e.g. Targets.tsx 'Macros' header, ProgressDashboard.tsx section overlines) — should render identically per the 'same element, same treatment' rule, in Inter sans, uppercase, tracked.
allFindings.note: Profile is a secondary (not daily-loop) surface, but the divergence is total — size (13px vs 11px), case (sentence vs upper), family (serif vs sans), and tracking (none vs 0.1em) all differ simultaneously from the app's dominant section-label pattern.

allFindings.title: Today's mid-scroll section headers render as 24px serif titles, not the mandated 11px tracked-caps overline — replicated on both platforms
allFindings.measured: Type.title applied to 'Activity & energy' / 'Hydration & stimulants' — Newsreader serif, 24px/28, weight 400, color navPrimary (mobile call sites: apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/_today/TodayScreen.tsx:4605 and :4697). Web mirror is byte-identical: src/app/components/suppr/today-scroll-section-header.tsx:32 renders 'text-2xl font-medium' serif (call sites NutritionTracker.tsx:2181 and :2253); web additionally reuses the same component for a THIRD, web-only structural label — the 'Today's Meals'/'Yesterday's Meals' title (src/app/components/suppr/today-meals-section.tsx:402-406, via mealsSectionTitle in src/lib/nutrition/trackerDate.ts:42).
allFindings.expected: Design Constitution Rule 1/5 (docs/ux/redesign/v3/DESIGN-CONSTITUTION.md): 'structural labels are the quiet tracked-caps overline... Serif never labels structure.' These are structural groupers (Activity & energy / Hydration & stimulants / Meals), not content nouns, so they should render as the 11px/700/uppercase/tracked label role, matching Type.label — not Type.title.
allFindings.note: Cross-platform consistent (both wrong the same way) except the web-only Meals title, so this is a systemic rule violation, not a parity bug — 5 instances total (2 native, 3 web).

allFindings.title: Web Settings renders all 8 of its section headers as 20px serif headlines; mobile Settings already fixed the identical labels to 11px tracked-caps overlines
allFindings.measured: 'Your plan' (927), 'Personal' (963), 'Preferences' (1077), 'Connections' (1660), 'Notifications' (1712), 'About' (1780), 'Promo code' (1805), 'Privacy & Security' (1868) — every one is `font-[family-name:var(--font-headline)] text-xl font-medium text-foreground-brand` (20px serif, brand-plum). Mobile's SectionHeading (apps/mobile/components/settings/SettingsBundleContent.tsx:163-186) renders its 12 equivalent section labels (Personal/Membership/People/Goals & targets/Display/Connections/Reminders/Recipes/Account/Legal/Build/Danger zone) at 11px/600/uppercase/0.9-tracking/textSecondary, with an explicit code comment: 'Was Newsreader serif plum 19px... uppercase eyebrow groups more aggressively.'
allFindings.expected: Same section content, same product, same screen family — should render identically. Mobile already migrated off the exact pattern web still uses; web is stuck on the pre-fix state the mobile comment describes as superseded. Constitution Rule 5 also directly forbids serif-as-section-head.
allFindings.note: This is a clean regression-style gap, not ambiguity: mobile's own comment documents the fix web never received. 8 instances on one screen.

allFindings.title: Plan, Recipes, and Progress each render a different legacy trailing-action-button shape today, live in production (the unifying flag defaults OFF)
allFindings.measured: Plan legacy `actLegacy`: 38×38, radius 12 (Radius.xl), 1px border + Elevation.cardHairline shadow, bg=card (mobile apps/mobile/components/plan/PlanHeaderV3.tsx:154-166; web src/app/components/plan/PlanHeaderV3.tsx:54 `h-[38px] w-[38px] rounded-xl border border-border bg-card shadow-sm`). Recipes legacy: bare icon, `{padding:6}`, NO background/border/shape at all (apps/mobile/components/tabs/RecipesTabChrome.tsx:33; web src/app/components/suppr/recipes-tab-chrome.tsx:54,64 `p-1.5 rounded-lg` hover-only). Progress legacy: 36×36 circle, 1px border, bg=card/elevated (apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/progress.tsx:1123-1131; web src/app/components/ProgressDashboard.tsx:1091 `h-9 w-9 rounded-full border border-border bg-card`).
allFindings.expected: Constitution Rule 3 / the ratified contract: 'Icon button: 40px circle, muted fill, ink glyph. There is no white-square, bordered, or shadowed variant. Anywhere.' All three converge on the identical 40px muted-circle treatment ONLY when `primary_screen_chrome_v1` is on — and it is registered DEFAULT-OFF on both platforms (apps/mobile/lib/analytics.ts:797; src/lib/analytics/track.ts:633), so today three adjacent daily-loop tabs each show a visibly different trailing-button treatment.
allFindings.note: This is the exact 'same element, same treatment' violation the project rules call out, and it is currently live, not a stale screenshot artifact.

allFindings.title: Settings has no overline eyebrow on either platform, breaking the contract's 3-line header template
allFindings.measured: Mobile: header row is back-chevron (30-40px depending on flag) + 'Settings' title only — no overline text node anywhere in the header block (apps/mobile/app/settings.tsx:120-164). Web: icon-plate (settings glyph in a plum-tinted circle) + 'Settings' title + a subtitle line ('Manage your account and preferences') — also no overline (src/app/components/Settings.tsx:855-876).
allFindings.expected: Contract (docs/decisions/2026-07-17-primary-ui-consistency-contract.md): Settings is named explicitly as one of the screens that must converge on 'quiet tracked-caps overline; 33px Newsreader page title; right-slot actions.' It has zero overline on either platform, and the two platforms don't even agree with each other on what replaces it (icon-plate+subtitle on web vs. bare back-button on mobile).
allFindings.note: Structural, not cosmetic — Settings' header composition doesn't match Plan/Recipes/Progress even in shape, independent of the flag.

allFindings.title: Desktop-web Recipes (Library) has no page-level title at all, unlike desktop Plan and Progress
allFindings.measured: grep for h1/page-title/text-3xl/consistencyChrome across Library.tsx returns nothing; the only serif headline-family text in the file is the empty-state message 'No saved recipes yet' (line 435, h2, content copy) and recipe-card titles (line 589, h3). RecipesTabChrome (which carries the overline+title) is `md:hidden` (src/app/components/suppr/screen-chrome.tsx:50) so it never renders on desktop.
allFindings.expected: Desktop Plan reuses the same PlanHeaderV3 as mobile-web (src/app/components/plan/PlanV3WebDashboard.tsx:290), and desktop Progress has its own page-title block (src/app/components/ProgressDashboard.tsx:1107, `page-title`/`text-3xl`). Recipes/Library should have an equivalent desktop title per the same convention; instead the content area starts directly with search/filter controls.
allFindings.note: This is the desktop counterpart of the ratified contract's own evidence line about Recipes/Progress chrome — but the desktop Recipes case is actually 'no title', not '24px vs 28px'.

allFindings.title: LogSheet's own title is a different size and weight on each platform
allFindings.measured: Mobile: `Type.title` = 24px / lineHeight 28 / weight 400 / -0.3 tracking, color navPrimary. Web: hardcoded `text-[22px] font-medium tracking-tight` = 22px / weight 500 (src/app/components/suppr/log-sheet.tsx:539).
allFindings.expected: Both files' own comments describe this as 'the same editorial-heading grammar as the Today section headers' — implying intended parity — but the literal values are 24/400 vs 22/500, a genuine cross-platform mismatch on the single most-used sheet in the app.

allFindings.title: Settings page title is a different size on each platform before the unifying flag ramps
allFindings.measured: Mobile default (flag off): `Type.title` = 24px. Web default (flag off): `text-3xl` = 28px (src/app/components/Settings.tsx:872; `--text-3xl: 1.75rem` per src/styles/theme.cssःcustom scale). Both converge on 33px (`Type.pageTitle`/`.page-title`) only once `primary_screen_chrome_v1` ramps.
allFindings.expected: Same screen, same title string ('Settings'), should render at one size on both platforms regardless of flag state — a screen this central shouldn't have a live 24-vs-28px split while waiting on a 0%-rolled-out flag.

allFindings.title: Plan's overline weight/tracking will diverge cross-platform the moment the consistency flag ramps
allFindings.measured: Mobile overline is `Type.statLabel` UNCONDITIONALLY (600 weight, 0.5 letterSpacing) — it does not fork on `consistencyChrome` at all. Web overline forks explicitly: `font-semibold tracking-[0.05em]` when off, `font-bold tracking-[0.1em]` when on (src/app/components/plan/PlanHeaderV3.tsx:74-77) — i.e. once flagged on, web's overline becomes 700-weight/0.1em-tracked while mobile's stays 600/0.5.
allFindings.expected: Both should read the same overline weight once the consistency chrome is the shipped state — mobile needs the same on/off fork web already has (or both should just always use `Type.label`/the 700-weight token, per the Constitution's single label role).
allFindings.note: Not yet visible in production (flag off), but it's baked into the code as it stands and will surface as a fresh divergence the day Grace ramps the flag rather than fixing the underlying convergence.

allFindings.title: FoodSearchPanel has 3 different raw caps-label sizes/weights within the same file, on each platform independently
allFindings.measured: Mobile: raw `fontSize:11, fontWeight:'700', letterSpacing:1` (lines 1714, 1772, 1825) vs raw `fontSize:10, fontWeight:'700', letterSpacing:0.8` (line 1862, 'If you log this') vs the one instance that actually spreads the canonical `Type.label` token (line 1896). Web: `text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider` = 12px/600 (lines 1913, 1938, 1982, 2012) vs `text-[11px] font-bold uppercase tracking-wider` = 11px/700 (lines 2187, 2243, 2311) vs `text-[10px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider` = 10px/600 (lines 2026, 2390) — none of the three web variants matches either of the two non-canonical mobile variants exactly.
allFindings.expected: One label role (Constitution Rule 5, role 6: 11px/700/uppercase/tracked/fg-tertiary) should cover every structural/eyebrow label in a component this information-dense; instead each platform independently reinvented 3 near-duplicates rather than importing the shared token.

allFindings.title: Mobile Settings' SectionHeading hand-rolls a near-duplicate of Type.label instead of importing it
allFindings.measured: fontFamily: FontFamily.sansSemibold, fontSize: 11, fontWeight: '600', color: colors.textSecondary, letterSpacing: 0.9 — vs the canonical `Type.label` (apps/mobile/constants/theme.ts:940-947): fontFamily sansBold, fontSize 11, fontWeight '700', letterSpacing 0.88, color intended as textTertiary.
allFindings.expected: Same visual intent (11px tracked uppercase eyebrow) should be the same token. This is the correct GRAMMAR (overline, not serif) but drifted off the actual `Type.label` values by weight (600 vs 700) and color (secondary vs tertiary) — a token-discipline gap even though the rule it's expressing is right.
allFindings.note: 12 call sites all inherit this single deviation via the shared SectionHeading component, so fixing it once fixes all 12.

allFindings.title: The ENG-1574/1577 chrome-unification flag defaults OFF on both platforms, so none of the ratified convergence is live
allFindings.measured: `primary_screen_chrome_v1` is listed inside `KNOWN_DEFAULT_OFF_FLAGS` on mobile (apps/mobile/lib/analytics.ts:797) and inside the equivalent `KNOWN_DEFAULT_OFF_FLAGS` on web (src/lib/analytics/track.ts:633) — neither is in the respective `REDESIGN_DEFAULT_ON` set, so `isFeatureEnabled` returns false absent an explicit PostHog rollout on both hosts.
allFindings.expected: Not itself a bug — this matches the project's own feature-flag policy (visual changes ship gated, ramp via PostHog) — but it means every legacy pattern documented above (P0/P1 findings) is the ACTUAL current production state on every screen this audit covers, not a stale pre-fix snapshot. Any visual/screenshot audit of these screens that didn't force the flag on is describing the pre-remediation reality, not the ratified target.
allFindings.note: Included as load-bearing context for interpreting any parallel visual-capture findings from the same sweep, not as an independent defect.

allFindings.title: Web Plan-tab option-pills still ship the pre-ENG-1521 ad-hoc 10% tint mobile explicitly fixed
allFindings.measured: active state = `border-primary/10 bg-primary/10 text-primary-solid` (ad-hoc 10% Tailwind opacity modifier), repeated at L1515 (day-count), L1561 (slot toggle), L1606 (start-date) — inline comments at L1506-1511/1553-1557/1599-1602 explicitly say this 'mirrors the mobile dayBtnActivePrimary (tint + "1A" border + fill)'
allFindings.expected: apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/planner.tsx:1831-1835 `dayBtnActivePrimary` was migrated in the ENG-1521 pass to `accent.primarySoft` (12% light / 18% dark) — the code comment there reads verbatim: 'primarySoft is the sanctioned Soft step... snapped up from the old ~10% bg-primary/10 parity'. Web's 'mirror' comment is now stale: it describes the pre-fix mobile behaviour, not the current one.
allFindings.note: Direct, code-documented proof of a cross-platform parity regression on a P0 daily-loop surface (Plan tab length/start/slot-toggle pills) — mobile fixed the exact issue web still has, and web's own comment cites the old, now-wrong mobile behaviour as its justification.

allFindings.title: 437 web chip/pill/badge fills use ad-hoc Tailwind slash-opacity instead of the sanctioned Soft/SoftStrong tokens, entirely untracked by the token-scale ratchet
allFindings.measured: grep -rE "bg-(primary|success|warning|destructive|muted|muted-foreground)/[0-9]{1,2}\b" src/app/components → 437 line-hits / 142 files, e.g. src/app/components/suppr/today-fasting-pill.tsx:52 (`bg-primary/10 hover:bg-primary/15`), src/app/components/suppr/today-hero-stats.tsx:251 (`bg-success/10`), src/app/components/suppr/upgrade-paywall-dialog.tsx:415,498,514 (`bg-primary/10`, `bg-primary/15` x2), src/app/components/imports/CsvImportPreview.tsx:60 (`bg-primary/10`), src/app/components/imports/NamedTrackerReassuranceStrip.tsx:27 (`bg-primary/15`), src/app/components/suppr/badge.tsx:39,45,48,50,53,55,58 (Badge primitive itself, `bg-warning/10`, `bg-success/10` etc.)
allFindings.expected: The sanctioned named tokens already exist and are correctly used elsewhere in the same tree (`bg-primary-soft` in filter-chip.tsx, import-detected-chip.tsx; `bg-slot-breakfast-soft` etc. in today-meals-section.tsx) — CSS confirms `--primary-soft: var(--accent-primary-soft) = rgba(91,59,110,0.12)` (theme.css:146,173). Per the UI write-discipline rule, an ad-hoc alpha is 'off-scale by construction' — this is the identical violation the ENG-1521 alpha-concat/withAlpha gate targets on mobile, just expressed via Tailwind's `/NN` syntax on a semantic colour name (which none of check-token-scale.mjs's HEX_RE/TW_RE/RGBA_RE/ALPHA_CONCAT_RE patterns match, since `primary`/`warning`/`success`/`muted` aren't in the TW_HUES palette-name list).
allFindings.note: Scale alone makes this the highest-leverage finding: it is larger than the entire currently-pinned token-scale debt (690 violations/151 files) and is completely unpinned, so it can grow silently on every new web PR without CI ever flagging it. Recommend extending check-token-scale.mjs with a 5th regex for `bg|text|border-(?:primary|success|warning|destructive|muted...)/\d{1,2}` before triaging remediation.

allFindings.title: Web TrustChip's 6 variants and ConfidenceChip render raw rgba() literals from the RETIRED indigo/green palette — mobile fixed the identical issue in the 2026-06-10 chips census
allFindings.measured: All 6 variant fills are untokenized rgba literals: L50 `rgba(98, 179, 90, 0.08)` (usda), L56 `rgba(76, 108, 224, 0.12)` (off-adjusted — blue/indigo), L62 `rgba(224, 168, 56, 0.10)` (estimated), L68 `rgba(140, 131, 120, 0.12)` (manual), L74 `rgba(98, 179, 90, 0.08)` (gluten-high-conf), L80 `rgba(224, 168, 56, 0.10)` (gluten-uncertain); confidence-chip.tsx:51 similarly hardcodes `rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.12)` (cool slate-blue).
allFindings.expected: apps/mobile/components/ui/TrustChip.tsx:44-84 resolves the same 6 variants via `Accent.successSoft`, `Accent.primarySoft`, `Accent.warningSoft`, `Colors.light.confidenceNeutralSoft` — token-derived from the current Sloe sage/aubergine/clay palette — with the explicit in-code annotation: 'Chips census (2026-06-10): token-derived wash — the rgba literals were the RETIRED green/indigo palette, not the sage/aubergine system.' Web never received this remediation; it is still rendering the exact retired blue/indigo `off-adjusted` fill mobile deliberately moved away from.
allFindings.note: Pinned as tracked legacy debt in scripts/token-budget.json (trust-chip.tsx: 6, confidence-chip.tsx: 1) so it will not silently grow further, but it is a live, currently-visible defect: every USDA/OFF/estimated/manual/gluten trust chip on web RecipeDetail and food-search surfaces renders off the wrong colour family versus its mobile counterpart.

allFindings.title: Same-role 'nutrition source' badge is a full pill on mobile but a 4px rounded-rect on web
allFindings.measured: web className: `rounded px-1.5 py-0.5 text-[10px]` — Tailwind `rounded` = 4px radius (not full), padding 6px/2px, single combined text-[10px] for abbr+label always shown together
allFindings.expected: apps/mobile/components/NutritionSourceBadge.tsx:45 `borderRadius: Radius.full` (999px, true pill), `paddingHorizontal: Spacing.xs` (4), `paddingVertical: 1`, separate abbr (fontSize 10) / label (fontSize 9) with a `compact` prop that hides the label by default — three measurable divergences (shape, padding, and default verbosity) for a component whose own header comment ('ENG-716... matching web's text-muted-foreground manual badge') claims parity.

allFindings.title: TrustChip/ConfidenceChip pill height disagrees 22px (mobile) vs 24px (web) — even the two files' own spec comments contradict each other
allFindings.measured: Mobile doc comment L14: 'Geometry: 22pt height, padding 3 x 8, radius Radius.full'; style at L120-129 sets `height: 22`. ConfidenceChip.tsx:60 same `height: 22`.
allFindings.expected: src/app/components/ui/trust-chip.tsx:18 doc comment: 'Pill geometry: 24px height, padding 3px x 8px, radius 999px'; implementation L108 `h-6` (=24px). confidence-chip.tsx:47 `h-6` likewise. Both platforms cite 'production design spec §1.6' as their source of truth yet land on different pixel heights for the identical pill.

allFindings.title: Shared Badge primitive: mobile renders label at weight 700, web at 600 (font-semibold) for the same variant set
allFindings.measured: mobile: `fontWeight: "700"` (label text style, L132)
allFindings.expected: src/app/components/suppr/badge.tsx:95 uses Tailwind `font-semibold` (600) on the identical primitive (both files' headers claim to mirror each other 1:1, 'same shape... so different labels no longer read as different apps glued together')

allFindings.title: Mobile Badge 'neutral'/'ai' variants use raw hex literals instead of a theme.ts token
allFindings.measured: L31 `neutral: "#94a3b8"` (slate-400, only commented as 'matches NutritionSourceBadge manual tone'), L36 `ai: "#9679D9"` (violet, commented 'Mirrors web --chart-5')
allFindings.expected: Every other variant in the same map resolves through `Accent.*` from theme.ts; these two anchor colours have no theme.ts token of their own despite theme.ts already defining a chart-5-equivalent and warm-grey provenance tokens (`sourceManual`) elsewhere.
allFindings.note: Already pinned as tracked legacy debt (token-budget.json: apps/mobile/components/Badge.tsx: 2) — cannot grow further under the ratchet, but flagged here since it sits in the same primitive audited above.

allFindings.title: Web meal-slot soft-tint computed two different ways in the same file for the same 12% result
allFindings.measured: A helper a few lines above (L188-194) returns ad-hoc `!bg-slot-lunch/12`, `!bg-slot-dinner/12`, `!bg-slot-snack/12` (forced `!important` Tailwind opacity-modifier)
allFindings.expected: A sibling helper directly below it (`slotHintClassName`, L200-214) returns the proper semantic class `bg-slot-lunch-soft` / `bg-slot-dinner-soft` / `bg-slot-snack-soft` for what is meant to be the identical 12% slot tint — two independent derivation paths for one design value inside one file means a future rebalance of the Soft percentage (theme.css) silently desyncs the `/12` call sites from the token-driven ones.
allFindings.note: Not currently visible (both paths happen to render 12% today) — a latent drift risk, not an active visual bug.

allFindings.title: Web core screens use three different page gutters — no single product-shell adoption
allFindings.measured: Targets.tsx:357 `max-w-5xl mx-auto px-pm-5 py-pm-5` = 32px gutter; RecipeDetail.tsx:1919 `max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-10` = 24px gutter (raw, un-tokenized Tailwind `px-6`, not `px-pm-*`); vs. 7 other core screens (NutritionTracker.tsx:1610, MealPlanner.tsx:1195, Library.tsx:239, ProgressDashboard.tsx:1203, Settings.tsx:2157, Profile.tsx:490, ShoppingList.tsx:196) all on `.product-shell` = 40px gutter (theme.css:967 `--spacing-pm-6`, theme.css:1179 `px-pm-6`). Distribution: 40px×7, 32px×1, 24px×1.
allFindings.expected: One gutter value per platform per the audit brief; all core page-ground screens should sit on `.product-shell` (or its gutter value) the way 7 of 9 already do.
allFindings.note: RecipeDetail additionally never widens past max-w-4xl on desktop (no xl:max-w-7xl), unlike product-shell — so it's narrower on desktop too, not just gutter-different.

allFindings.title: Web core screens are saturated with off-scale fractional/bracket Tailwind spacing, with zero CI gate covering it
allFindings.measured: 121 total off-scale spacing-utility instances counted across the 9 examined web core screens (grep for m*/p*/gap*/space-*-{[Npx]|N.5} patterns): RecipeDetail.tsx 28, Profile.tsx 24, MealPlanner.tsx 18, ProgressDashboard.tsx 15, Settings.tsx 14, Library.tsx 10, Targets.tsx 7, ShoppingList.tsx 3, NutritionTracker.tsx 2. 19 of these are exactly `-2.5` (10px) — e.g. RecipeDetail.tsx:3502 `gap-2.5`, Settings.tsx:906 `gap-2.5`, Library.tsx:441 `gap-2.5`, ShoppingList.tsx:311 `py-2.5`/`gap-1.5`, Profile.tsx:549/578/744/786 `mt-[22px] mb-2.5` (repeated section-header pattern, 4 identical instances). scripts/check-spacing-scale.mjs header comment (lines ~21-23) states scope is 'apps/mobile/app + apps/mobile/components .tsx only ... web uses the Tailwind spacing scale, gated separately' — but no such web gate exists in scripts/ (only check-type-scale.mjs for font sizes and check-web-radius.mjs for radius were found).
allFindings.expected: Web spacing should snap to 4/8/12/16/20/24/32/40 the same as mobile (per root CLAUDE.md 'Tokens only' rule naming both theme.css + Tailwind theme as the legal source), and should have an equivalent only-shrink ratchet the way mobile does.
allFindings.note: The 10px gap (-2.5) is the literal example the design contract itself names as a bug ('An 18px padding or 10px gap is a bug even if it looks fine') — this is not a hypothetical, it is directly instantiated 19 times.

allFindings.title: Mobile screen-frame gutter also splits in two — Today/Plan at 20px vs 8 other core screens at 24px
allFindings.measured: Layout.todayScreenPaddingX (layout.ts:28) and Layout.planScreenPaddingX (layout.ts:40) both = Spacing.lg = 20px, consumed at TodayScreen.tsx:2897 and planner.tsx:1605. Every other examined core screen uses Spacing.xl = 24px: library.tsx:557 (Recipes), progress.tsx:1107 (Layout.screenPaddingX), settings.tsx:94 (body), recipe/[id].tsx:1680/1698 (body), paywall.tsx:873, targets.tsx:427/452, profile.tsx:196, shopping.tsx:569 (Layout.screenPaddingX). Distribution: 24px×8, 20px×2.
allFindings.expected: One gutter per platform, or if Today/Plan's tighter density is a deliberate ratified exception, Recipes should join it — Recipes is the third of the three primary swipeable tabs but sits at the 'secondary screen' 24px value instead.
allFindings.note: layout.ts:12-16 documents the Today/Plan 20px choice as intentional prototype-density parity, but that rationale was never extended to Recipes, which is the same tier of screen.

allFindings.title: Web Today inverts the spacing hierarchy: gap between top-level sections is smaller than the gap inside a section
allFindings.measured: Outer container `space-y-4` (16px, NutritionTracker.tsx:1610) governs the gap between top-level siblings including the 'Activity & energy' and 'Hydration & stimulants' `<section>` blocks (lines 2179, 2251), while each of those sections uses `flex flex-col gap-5` (20px) internally between its header and cards (lines 2179, 2251).
allFindings.expected: Gaps between distinct semantic sections should be ≥ the gap inside a section (the mobile TodayScreen gets this right: top-level scroll `gap: Spacing.xl`=24, TodayScreen.tsx:2899, > `Layout.todaySectionCardGap`=20, TodayScreen.tsx:4602/4694).
allFindings.note: This reads as a real hierarchy/legibility bug, not just an off-token nitpick: adjacent unrelated sections visually read as more grouped together than a section header reads with its own content.

allFindings.title: Section-rhythm mechanism itself is inconsistent — some screens use one container gap token, others hand-roll per-child margins that drift within a single screen
allFindings.measured: Settings.tsx:856 title block uses `mb-8` (32px); the very next elements — proBanner (Settings.tsx:904) and planCard (Settings.tsx:924), both `SupprCard ... mb-6` (24px) — use a different, smaller gap. No shared gap token connects them. Compare to Today/Plan/Recipes/Shopping (web), which each use ONE `space-y-N`/`gap-N` at the container level (NutritionTracker.tsx:1610 `space-y-4`, MealPlanner.tsx:1195 `space-y-5`, Library.tsx:239 `space-y-5`, ShoppingList.tsx:196 `space-y-5`) so their inter-section gap can't drift mid-screen. Mobile Paywall (paywall.tsx) has the same problem — `scrollContent` (paywall.tsx:873) carries no `gap`, and children set their own `marginBottom`/`marginTop` ad hoc (e.g. heroBleed marginBottom: Spacing.lg=20 at paywall.tsx:865, timelineWrap marginTop: Spacing.xl=24 at paywall.tsx:900).
allFindings.expected: Every screen should establish inter-section rhythm via one container-level gap token, not per-child margins — the pattern already used correctly on 4 of 9 web screens and most mobile screens.
allFindings.note: ProgressDashboard.tsx, Targets.tsx, and Profile.tsx (web) share this same ad-hoc-margin pattern as Settings — 5 of 9 web screens total.

allFindings.title: Mobile RecipeDetail has a third, orphaned gutter value between its hero and its body cards
allFindings.measured: The conditional 'Generate an image' CTA row sits in `{ paddingHorizontal: Spacing.md, paddingTop: Spacing.sm }` (16px gutter, recipe/[id].tsx:2099), sandwiched between the full-bleed hero and `styles.body` (24px gutter via `Spacing.xl`, recipe/[id].tsx:1680, applied at line 2110).
allFindings.expected: Every element below the full-bleed hero should share the same 24px (Spacing.xl) gutter as the rest of the page body.
allFindings.note: Only visible when `canGenerateSloeHero` is true (AI-hero-eligible recipes without an existing image) — a conditional, not universal, drift.

allFindings.title: Mobile Settings header row sits 4px narrower than the screen body it introduces
allFindings.measured: Header row (back chevron + 'Settings' title) uses `paddingHorizontal: Spacing.lg` = 20px (settings.tsx:131). The ScrollView body directly below — search input row and settings list — uses `paddingHorizontal: Spacing.xl` = 24px (settings.tsx:94, muted style at settings.tsx:107).
allFindings.expected: Header and body gutter should match on the same screen so the back chevron and the content below it align to the same left edge.

allFindings.title: On-scale spacing values hand-typed as raw literals instead of the named token, on core screens
allFindings.measured: discover.tsx:703 `paddingHorizontal: 32` (legal value, should be `Spacing.xxl`). Web Settings.tsx:2157 `product-shell py-8` (32px raw Tailwind — every other product-shell consumer uses the semantic `py-pm-N` utility, e.g. Profile.tsx:490 `py-pm-5`, MealPlanner.tsx:1195 `py-pm-6`). Web RecipeDetail.tsx:1919 `px-6 py-10` (24px/40px raw Tailwind, not `px-pm-6`/`py-pm-6`).
allFindings.expected: Per the 'tokens only' rule, on-scale values should still resolve through the named token/utility, not a bare literal — this is what lets a future scale change propagate everywhere.

allFindings.title: Mobile core-screen off-scale spacing literals are ratcheted/bounded; the equivalent web debt (see web-tailwind-fractional-spacing finding) has no such gate
allFindings.measured: Pinned off-scale counts for the core screens examined: apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/planner.tsx: 10 (one confirmed instance at planner.tsx:1884 `paddingHorizontal: 14`), apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/_today/TodayScreen.tsx: 5, apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/discover.tsx: 4, apps/mobile/app/shopping.tsx: 4, apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/progress.tsx: 2, apps/mobile/app/targets.tsx: 3, apps/mobile/app/profile.tsx: 3, apps/mobile/app/paywall.tsx: 1. These can only shrink (only-shrink ratchet, check:spacing-scale). No equivalent budget/ratchet file exists for web spacing.
allFindings.expected: This is presented as corroborating evidence, not a new defect — cited so the remediation program treats mobile debt as bounded and treats the web-spacing gap (already reported) as the higher-priority remediation target.

allFindings.title: Shared SupprButton CTA label renders 13px on web vs 16px on mobile — every primary/ghost button product-wide
allFindings.measured: Web: base cva string `"...text-sm font-medium..."` is never overridden past font-weight by suppr-button.tsx's `className="px-5 font-semibold"` (twMerge only swaps font-medium→font-semibold, size stays text-sm). `--text-sm: 0.8125rem` = 13px (src/styles/theme.css:23). Height fixed at Button size="lg" → h-10 = 40px, no size variation. Mobile: apps/mobile/components/ui/SupprButton.tsx uses `Type.button` = fontSize 16/lineHeight 20/weight 600 (theme.ts:938), height varies by `size` prop: md ≈52px (paddingVertical 16×2 + lineHeight 20), sm ≈36px (paddingVertical 8×2 + lineHeight 20).
allFindings.expected: Both platforms render the documented '2 variants only' primitive identically per its own parity comment ("Web mirror... so the two platforms read identically"). Web should use a 16px/600 label size (matching Type.button) and expose the same md/sm height tiers mobile has, not a single fixed 40px/13px rendering.
allFindings.surface: Today, Plan, Recipes, Progress, RecipeDetail, Log — every SupprButton instance

allFindings.title: Log-entry flow (FAB → Quick add/Voice/Photo/Custom-food) concentrates 34 of 114 legacy feedbackless raw Pressables
allFindings.measured: QuickAddPanel.tsx: 5 feedbackless `<Pressable>` (lines 555,632,676,731,786). VoiceLogSheet.tsx: 8 (230,234,266,284,407,425,442,458). PhotoLogSheet.tsx: 11 (481,485,516,591,609,742,805,883,901,918,934). CreateCustomFoodSheet.tsx: 10 (754,762,807,956,1029,1043,1154,1347,1482,1501). Plus Today-adjacent TodayWeekView.tsx: 3 (282,416,533) and HydrationStimulantsCard.tsx: 5 (222,277,296,307,343). Total 42/114 (37%) of the ratchet's current legacy census sits on the single highest-frequency daily-loop entry surface. Confirmed via live run of scripts/check-pressable-feedback.mjs `scanTree()` — matches scripts/pressable-feedback-budget.json exactly (CI green).
allFindings.expected: Per the non-negotiable rule, every Pressable routes through `PressableScale` with the right haptic weight. These are pinned as only-shrink legacy debt, not remediated — the Log flow (the core capture loop) should be prioritized first given tap frequency, not left as the largest unaddressed slice.
allFindings.note: Already tracked by the check:pressable-feedback ratchet (not a silent gap) — flagged here for prioritization given its concentration on the core daily-loop surface.

allFindings.title: RecipeDetail web 'Generate an image' CTA bypasses SupprButton, uses bg-primary instead of bg-primary-solid — diverges from mobile and from every other primary CTA in dark mode
allFindings.measured: Web: raw `<button className="...rounded-full bg-primary px-4 py-2 text-sm...">` (RecipeDetail.tsx:1957-1964), not `<SupprButton>`. `--accent-primary` (dark) = #7E5C92 vs `--accent-primary-solid` (dark) = #C4ACD0 (src/styles/theme.css:535,538) — visibly different fills in dark mode; coincidentally equal in light mode (#3B2A4D both). Padding px-4/py-2 (16px/8px) vs the SupprButton primitive's px-5/h-10 (20px/40px). Mobile equivalent correctly uses `<SupprButton variant="primary">` (apps/mobile/app/recipe/[id].tsx:2100-2106), which resolves to `accent.primarySolid`.
allFindings.expected: Route through `<SupprButton variant="primary">` (or at minimum swap to `bg-primary-solid`/`text-primary-foreground`) to match every other primary CTA on the page and the mobile counterpart, eliminating the dark-mode-only color divergence.

allFindings.title: Web SupprButton has no size prop (mobile has size='md'|'sm') — call sites hand-override height/padding, producing ≥4 different rendered CTA heights off the spacing scale
allFindings.measured: Interface only accepts `variant`/`loading`/`label`/`children`/native button props — no `size`. Consumers override via `className`: north-star-block.tsx:340 `"h-9"` (36px, vs component default 40px); MealPlanner.tsx:2338 `"h-8 px-2 text-[11px]"` (32px + untracked arbitrary label size); CookMode.tsx:804 `"h-auto gap-1.5 px-2 py-0.5 text-base"` (gap-1.5=6px, py-0.5=2px — both off the 4/8/12/16/20/24/32/40 scale); CookMode.tsx:841 `"h-auto gap-1.5 px-2 py-0.5 mx-0.5 text-[0.9em] align-baseline"` (same off-scale spacing + an em-relative arbitrary type value). Mobile's SupprButton by contrast exposes a real `size?: "md"|"sm"` prop using only `Spacing.sm`/`Spacing.md` tokens (theme.ts-backed, no ad-hoc overrides needed).
allFindings.expected: Add a documented `size` prop (mirroring mobile's md/sm, or a 3rd 'compact' token) so call sites stop reaching for one-off Tailwind overrides. None of these off-scale values (gap-1.5, py-0.5, mx-0.5) are caught by any ratchet — `check:spacing-scale` is mobile-only per root CLAUDE.md, and `check:type-scale` only matches literal `text-[Npx]`, not `text-[0.9em]` — so this drift is currently untracked, not just legacy.

allFindings.title: Ad-hoc buttons bypassing Button/SupprButton ship with no :focus-visible state, incl. 2 monetization 'Upgrade' CTAs
allFindings.measured: UpgradePrompt.tsx:62 `className="...rounded-xl bg-primary text-primary-foreground..."` (filled Upgrade CTA) — no focus-visible class. UpgradePrompt.tsx:117 (compact Upgrade CTA) — same gap. Also confirmed feedback-less on: DayStrip.tsx:305 (calendar-open icon), Targets.tsx:557 (macro-tile edit tap target), suppr/today-recents-row.tsx:48 ("Log your first meal" empty-state CTA), suppr/hydration-stimulants-card.tsx:134 (row overflow menu), plan/PlanMealCardV3.tsx:147 (meal-card options), RecipeUpload.tsx:1805 & :1825 ("Import instead"/"Clear form"), Library.tsx:392,464,479 (sort toggle, "Create your own", "Explore Discover"). 0/12 sampled ad-hoc buttons carry `focus-visible:` classes. Contrast: suppr/today-snap-shortcut.tsx:52, suppr/log-hub-quick-actions.tsx:71, plan/PlanHeaderV3.tsx:46, and library/FeaturedHero.tsx:50 all correctly ship `focus-visible:ring-2` — proving the pattern is known, just inconsistently applied.
allFindings.expected: Every interactive control ships `:focus-visible` per the non-negotiable 'states ship with the element' rule — either route through `Button`/`SupprButton` (which bake this in) or add the ring utility directly, as the 4 correct examples above already do.

allFindings.title: Library.tsx duplicates the same hand-rolled secondary-button markup twice instead of a shared component
allFindings.measured: Lines 464-477 ("Create your own") and 479-487 ("Explore Discover") share the byte-identical `className="w-full bg-background border border-border text-foreground font-semibold text-sm rounded-full py-3 inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 hover:bg-muted/60 transition-colors"` (234 chars, confirmed via exact-string grep — 2 occurrences, 0 other file in src/app). Representative of a broader 91-site population of ad-hoc `<button>`s across src/app carrying their own bg+radius+padding combo instead of `Button`/`SupprButton` (static scan across ~60 files).
allFindings.expected: Extract one outline/secondary sub-component (or add a 3rd SupprButton variant) so the two can't drift independently on a future edit — this is the Recipes tab's empty state, a core-screen surface.

allFindings.title: Sole remaining raw TouchableOpacity in the mobile app
allFindings.measured: 1 file uses `TouchableOpacity` — every other interactive control in apps/mobile has migrated to `Pressable`/`PressableScale` (509 raw Pressable occurrences, 257 PressableScale usages; TouchableOpacity count = 1 file, 0 elsewhere).
allFindings.expected: Migrate to `Pressable`/`PressableScale` for haptic + scale parity with the rest of the app, and to stop this pattern being copy-pasted forward.
allFindings.surface: generic disclosure component (not a core-6 screen)

allFindings.title: Web TrustChip ships the exact retired slate/blue/green palette that a dated mobile-only fix already replaced
allFindings.measured: usda bg=rgba(98,179,90,0.08); off-adjusted bg=rgba(76,108,224,0.12) [BLUE — no blue hue exists anywhere in the ratified Sloe 6-hue palette]; estimated/gluten-uncertain bg=rgba(224,168,56,0.10); manual bg=rgba(140,131,120,0.12) [warm grey]
allFindings.expected: Accent.successSoft / Accent.primarySoft / Accent.warningSoft / Colors.light.confidenceNeutralSoft — exactly what apps/mobile/components/ui/TrustChip.tsx now uses, per its own comment: "Chips census (2026-06-10): token-derived wash — the rgba literals were the RETIRED green/indigo palette, not the sage/aubergine system" (commit 9c32d3db)
allFindings.note: Mobile mirror apps/mobile/components/ui/TrustChip.tsx:38-77 was fixed 2026-06-10 (commit 9c32d3db) and again touched in the ENG-1521 soft-tint pass (96f5f134); web was never updated. Web and mobile now render six visibly different colors for the same six semantic trust-chip variants on the same surfaces.

allFindings.title: Web ConfidenceChip hardcodes Tailwind slate-400 instead of the warm confidence-neutral token, contradicting its own doc comment
allFindings.measured: backgroundColor: "rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.12)" (slate-400, a cool blue-grey)
allFindings.expected: var(--confidence-neutral) at the soft-tint step (~#6A6072 mauve-grey) — the component's own comment claims "Background is the neutral `--confidence-neutral` token at 10%" but the code never reads that variable at all
allFindings.surface: adaptive TDEE display, food-search confidence indicators
allFindings.note: apps/mobile/components/ui/ConfidenceChip.tsx:40-42 carries the identical dated fix: "Chips census (2026-06-10): warm neutral wash — the slate literal was a cool-grey outlier on a warm-aubergine app" then uses colors.confidenceNeutralSoft. Web's comment describes the fixed behavior; web's code still has the bug.

allFindings.title: SearchResultConfidenceChip "verified" tier renders a raw blue with no corresponding Sloe-palette token
allFindings.measured: verified: { bg: "rgba(88, 140, 228, 0.12)", fg: Accent.primary } — 88,140,228 = #588CE4, a saturated blue with zero other consumers anywhere in theme.ts/theme.css
allFindings.expected: A hue from the ratified Sloe 6-hue family (plum/clay/sage/amber/damson/teal) — theme.css's own palette-intro comment states the family "Replaces the blue 8-slot lock"; this chip is the one place blue survived
allFindings.surface: barcode scan result + voice log result (Log tab — a named core daily-loop surface)
allFindings.note: The component's own comment admits it: "Prototype colours: Verified = soft-blue... Mapped onto the mobile tokens" — the mapping never happened, it just kept the prototype blue. fg uses Accent.primary (clay) as icon/text color ON the blue background, an additional hue mismatch within the same chip.

allFindings.title: TodayHero "On track" pill hardcodes a different, brighter green than the sage success token — and its own comment is factually wrong
allFindings.measured: const SUCCESS_SOFT = "rgba(86, 167, 117, 0.12)" (86,167,117 = #56A775); comment claims "no `Accent.successSoft` token exists"
allFindings.expected: Accent.successSoft, which DOES exist (theme.ts ~L116: withAlpha('#5E7C5A', SOFT)) and is what every other success-state chip in the app renders. #56A775 vs sage #5E7C5A differs by +43 in the green channel alone — a visibly different, more saturated kelly-green vs the muted olive sage used everywhere else
allFindings.note: #56A775 has been in this file since commit 94fb5b7b/32402a23 (pre-dates the 2026-06-03 Sloe rebrand) — a pre-rebrand green that was never migrated while every other Accent.success consumer was.

allFindings.title: Web Today confetti + Household section-eyebrow labels each define a third and fourth distinct 'sage/success' green
allFindings.measured: confetti colors: ["#56A775", "#F3C336", "#DF5EBC", "#5e574e"] — same #56A775 as TodayHero.tsx above; separately, src/app/components/HouseholdSettingsPage.tsx:438,535,565,608 hardcode text-[#7C8466] labeled "sage #7C8466" in a code comment — a THIRD green (124,132,102) matching neither #5E7C5A nor #56A775
allFindings.expected: One sage identity (#5E7C5A / Accent.success) product-wide per the 'same element, same treatment' rule; section-eyebrow labels should use the neutral overline ink per the Design Constitution label grammar, not an ad-hoc green
allFindings.note: Confetti itself is low-stakes/decorative (P3 in isolation), but it's evidence the #56A775 leftover is copy-pasted across at least 2 files; the HouseholdSettingsPage eyebrow is a structural section label rendered in a bespoke green nowhere else in the design system.

allFindings.title: Plan tab 'estimated macros' chip references a CSS custom property that does not exist — fallback color always renders instead of the design token
allFindings.measured: color: "var(--accent-warning, #b8860b)", backgroundColor: "var(--accent-warning-bg, rgba(232, 160, 32, 0.12))" — `--accent-warning-bg` is not defined anywhere in src/styles/theme.css (only `--accent-warning-soft` is), so this backgroundColor ALWAYS resolves to the raw fallback rgba(232,160,32,0.12) [#E8A020]
allFindings.expected: var(--accent-warning-soft) = rgba(201, 137, 44, 0.12) [#C9892C] — the token every other amber/warning chip in the app uses
allFindings.note: Reproducible every time meal.macrosAreEstimated is true (P1-19 recipe-verify flow). The color fallback (#b8860b, CSS darkgoldenrod) is dead code since --accent-warning does exist, but the background fallback is live and wrong on every render.

allFindings.title: Onboarding pace-warning banner shows two different reds/ambers side-by-side — outer fill hardcoded, inner icon chip correctly tokenised
allFindings.measured: danger: { bg: "rgba(217,69,69,0.12)", border: "rgba(217,69,69,0.45)", accent: "var(--destructive)" }; warn: { bg: "rgba(232,148,45,0.12)", ... } — the banner's own fill/border never reference --accent-destructive-soft (rgba(176,68,52,0.12)) or --accent-warning-soft
allFindings.expected: var(--accent-destructive-soft) / var(--accent-warning-soft) for bg+border so the banner fill matches its own accent color family
allFindings.note: Mobile mirror apps/mobile/components/onboarding/steps/pace.tsx:406-413 has the identical hardcode (rgba(217,69,69,...) / rgba(232,148,45,...)) for the banner bg/border, while 2 lines below it computes `accentSoft: Accent.destructiveSoftStrong` / `Accent.warningSoftStrong` and uses THAT correct token only for the small 28x28 icon-chip nested inside the same banner (line ~448) — two visibly different reds/ambers stacked in one component, on both platforms identically.

allFindings.title: Targets screen page-ground cards combine border AND shadow — the exact double-separation anti-pattern ENG-1497 prohibits
allFindings.measured: className="bg-card border rounded-2xl p-5 shadow-sm ..." (also line 502, the Goal card, identical pattern) — rounded-2xl is also 16px, off the 24px card-corner token
allFindings.expected: Per docs/decisions/2026-07-10-card-grammar-rounder-flat.md: page-ground cards = hairline border OR fill contrast, never both border and shadow ("a flat white card on a near-white ground... not reintroducing shadow"); radius should be rounded-card (24)
allFindings.note: The rounded-2xl radius drift is already tracked debt (scripts/web-radius-budget.json pins Targets.tsx at 4 legacy hits), but the shadow-sm co-occurring with border is NOT caught by any ratchet — check:web-radius only checks radius, check:token-scale only checks hex/rgba/tailwind-class. No automated gate exists for 'no shadow on a page-ground card' on web.

allFindings.title: 'Why this number' result card is a tinted nested card that still carries a shadow
allFindings.measured: className="flex items-center gap-3 rounded-2xl bg-primary px-4 py-4 text-primary-foreground shadow-md" data-testid="why-number-result-card"
allFindings.expected: Per the same ENG-1497 ruling: "Tinted cards (import banner, empty-state cards) are already flat — no border added; the tint IS the separation" — a primary-filled card should drop the shadow entirely, doubly so since this renders nested inside the why-this-number sheet (nested cards = flat, borderless)
allFindings.note: Also off-token rounded-2xl (16px) instead of the 24px card corner; already pinned once in scripts/web-radius-budget.json for this file.

allFindings.title: Elevation.cardSoft's own token-file documentation is stale post-ENG-1497, contradicting the hook that consumes it
allFindings.measured: Block comment above `cardSoft` still reads "As of 2026-06-04 this is the DEFAULT light resting-card treatment (useCardElevation no longer gates it behind the flag...)" — no mention of the 2026-07-10 ENG-1497 supersession anywhere in this comment block; Colors.light comments at theme.ts:608/616 similarly still say "white cards + Elevation.cardSoft" / "lift via Elevation.cardSoft"
allFindings.expected: apps/mobile/hooks/useCardElevation.ts:16-22 has the accurate, up-to-date comment: "SOFT is the page-ground card treatment... cards are FLAT + hairline — no ambient shadow... `Elevation.cardSoft` survives for sheets/overlays only" — theme.ts's own comment should match
allFindings.note: No live call site currently misuses Elevation.cardSoft on a page-ground card (verified — every non-comment reference is Elevation.card, a neutral no-op, or a genuine sheet/toast). Purely a documentation-drift risk: a future engineer reading theme.ts in isolation would reintroduce the shadow believing it's still the sanctioned default.

allFindings.title: Plan header icon button renders two entirely different chrome treatments for the same element, gated by a boolean prop
allFindings.measured: consistencyChrome ? "h-10 w-10 rounded-full bg-muted" : "h-[38px] w-[38px] rounded-xl border border-border bg-card shadow-sm" — flat pill vs. bordered+shadowed square, same button role
allFindings.expected: Per 'same element, same treatment': one chrome definition for the Plan header icon button, not a fork; if the flag genuinely represents an in-flight migration it should be flagged per the feature-flag rule, not left as permanent dual-path code

allFindings.title: Household settings + IngredientImageTile use a warm off-token cream that isn't the cool-toned muted/skeleton token
allFindings.measured: #ECEAE4 (warm cream, 236,234,228) — duplicated identically at apps/mobile/components/IngredientImageTile.tsx:54
allFindings.expected: --muted / Colors.*.skeleton (#EBEAF1, a cool plum-frost grey, 235,234,241) — same lightness, different hue family (warm vs cool), a near-token drift

allFindings.title: WeeklyRecapCard's standalone 'artifact palette' PLUM_C is 1-2 hex steps off --primary-deep

allFindings.title: SVG gradient stops in the calorie ring duplicate token hex values as brittle literals
allFindings.measured: stopColor="#3B2A4D" / "#C8794E" / "#C9892C" (winSpectrum) and "#3B2A4D" / "#5B3B6E" / "#7E5C92" (ringEmptyGradient) — hardcoded, value-equal to --accent-win-gradient stops today
allFindings.expected: Documented as unavoidable (SVG stroke can't consume a CSS linear-gradient() or custom property), but there is no test pinning these three stops to the token so a future --accent-win-gradient edit would silently desync the ring from the token it claims to mirror
allFindings.note: Zero current visual drift — flagging the maintenance/regression risk, not a present-day inconsistency. Mobile CalorieRing equivalent should be checked for the same literal-duplication pattern and a shared constant/test-pin considered.

allFindings.title: Onboarding 'weight' step and its blue accent have no home in the Sloe palette

allFindings.title: planner.tsx — largest weighted debt in the codebase, core Plan surface
allFindings.measured: 4,754 lines (4,354 over the 400-line screen budget ≈ 44 size-pts) + 10 pinned off-scale spacing literals + 10 pinned raw colour/radius literals. Raw=64, budgets hit=3/5 (spacing, token, screen-lines), core=Plan → weighted=64×3=192.
allFindings.expected: 0 pinned literals; screen split via a usePlanner() hook / child components under 400 lines per CLAUDE.md's '3,400-line index.tsx is legacy, not a target' rule; spacing on 4/8/12/16/20/24/32/40; colours/radii via Colors/Radius tokens.

allFindings.title: TodayScreen.tsx — CLAUDE.md's named legacy file has grown to 5,912 lines
allFindings.measured: 5,912 lines (5,512 over budget ≈ 55 size-pts) + 5 off-scale spacing + 2 raw colour/radius literals. Raw=62, budgets=3/5, core=Today → weighted=62×3=186.

allFindings.title: Every specialist agent's STEP ZERO context read points to a nonexistent path
allFindings.measured: "Always start by reading `/Users/graceturner/Suppr-1/.claude/agents/_project-context.md`" — verified via `ls`: this path does not exist (ENOENT). The real repo root is `/Users/gracehowse/sloe`. Identical broken reference appears in all 36 files under `.claude/agents/` that cite the file, including ui-product-designer.md:18, design-system-enforcer.md:20, visual-qa.md:18, design-director.md:29, premium-auditor.md:156, orchestrator.md:16, executor.md:18, brand-manager.md:18, code-quality.md:18, sync-enforcer.md:19, planner.md:16, release-gate.md:18, and 24 others (full list available via `grep -rln "graceturner/Suppr-1" .claude/agents/*.md`).
allFindings.expected: Every reference should read `.claude/agents/_project-context.md` (relative) or the correct absolute path under `/Users/gracehowse/sloe/`, so the file that centralises cross-cutting rules (competitor set, trust posture, canonical scales, card grammar, intentional divergences) is actually loadable by the agent reading its own instructions.
allFindings.note: This is the single largest root-cause: it is the mechanism by which every other piece of centralised context in _project-context.md fails to reach any specialist agent, not just the design ones. One `sed -i` across .claude/agents/*.md fixes all 36 sites at once — worth root-causing as one fix, not 36 separate PRs.

allFindings.title: _project-context.md's Radius scale omits the ENG-1497 card-radius value
allFindings.measured: "**Radius:** 4 / 6 / 8 / 12 / full (`Radius` — 2026-05-22 lock; bigger reads 'kids' tablet')" — 5 values, no 24.
allFindings.expected: 4 / 6 / 8 / 12 / 24 / full, matching root CLAUDE.md L98 ("Radius snaps to: 4 / 6 / 8 / 12 / 24 / full ... THE card corner — ENG-1497, 2026-07-10"), AGENTS.md L89-90 (identical), DESIGN-CONSTITUTION.md Rule 2 ("radius 24"), and theme.ts:839 (`card: 24`). Because _project-context.md is the file every other agent is told to read as ground truth, this is the most consequential of the radius-scale sites: it actively teaches every specialist agent that 24 is illegal.

allFindings.expected: Should match root CLAUDE.md L98 (4/6/8/12/24/full) exactly — this file is explicitly the mobile-scoped sibling of root CLAUDE.md, auto-loaded for every task under apps/mobile/, so a mobile engineer or agent styling a card sees a DIFFERENT legal radius set than the one two directories up.

allFindings.expected: Spacing 4/8/12/16/20/24/32/40; Radius 4/6/8/12/24/full — matching CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, theme.ts. This file was last touched 2026-07-12 (after BOTH the spacing-dense and card-radius rulings landed), so the staleness isn't age — it's a restatement that was never reconciled against its own source of truth even when the file was edited for other reasons.

allFindings.title: visual-qa.md's inline canonical-scales restatement omits radius 24 AND spacing 12 — the exact same two omissions as design-director.md

None of the 4 named design agents (ui-critic, ui-product-designer, design-system-enforcer, visual-qa) — nor design-director.md — reference `Sloe-App.html`, `redesign/v3`, or `DESIGN-CONSTITUTION.md` anywhere (confirmed by grep across the entire `.claude/agents/` directory).

Running this agent today would produce systematically backwards verdicts against a Grace-ratified redesign.

allFindings.title: Root CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, DESIGN-CONSTITUTION.md, and the shipped SupprButton code specify three different, mutually incompatible secondary-button treatments
allFindings.measured: CLAUDE.md L103-104 / AGENTS.md L110-111 (byte-identical): "**One filled CTA per screen** (FAB + conversion surfaces excepted — see the 2026-06-09 CTA decision). Secondary = outline, tertiary = ghost."  vs.  DESIGN-CONSTITUTION.md L84 (Rule 3, claimed ratified 2026-07-17): "Buttons: one filled CTA per screen (conversion surfaces excepted) · secondary = tonal · tertiary = ghost text. Unchanged from the CTA ruling."  vs.  the actual shipped type `apps/mobile/components/ui/SupprButton.tsx:41`: `export type SupprButtonVariant = "primary" | "ghost";` (mirrored on web, `src/app/components/ui/button.tsx`) — only two variants exist in code, and per the governing decision `docs/decisions/2026-06-12-button-system-solid-primary.md` ("Grace approved the red-line 2026-06-12"), item 2: "**Secondary → ghost.** ... Replaces both the outline AND the beige `colors.card` fill" — outline was explicitly retired, and no 'tonal' variant was ever specified or built.
allFindings.expected: One statement, consistent everywhere, matching the shipped code: primary = solid fill (exactly one per screen, FAB/conversion excepted), secondary = ghost (transparent, no border) — there is no third tier and no 'outline' or 'tonal' variant. CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md's 'outline' is stale (the outline treatment was the thing 2026-06-12 replaced); DESIGN-CONSTITUTION.md's 'tonal' describes a variant that was never implemented; both also imply a nonexistent third ('tertiary') tier the button primitive doesn't support.
allFindings.note: Also present verbatim in AGENTS.md:110-111 (the mirror). This is the most concrete, code-verifiable contradiction in the audit — it's not a documentation nuance, it's three different instructions for a rule every screen in the app invokes, and none of the three matches what actually ships.

allFindings.title: theme.ts's Radius section header comment was never updated when Radius.card (24) was added
allFindings.measured: Header comment: "Canonical 2026-05-22 lock — tighter radii... Tightening to 8/6/4/12 cascades through ~287 callers without further edits.\n// Previous values: sm 8, md 12, lg 16, xl 20" — makes no mention of the `card: 24` property added seven weeks later at L834-839 (ENG-1497, 2026-07-10), which carries its own separate inline comment.
allFindings.expected: The section header should be updated alongside additions to the object it describes, so a reader skimming only the block comment (rather than every individual property doc) isn't left with an incomplete picture of the legal radius set. Low severity because the correct value (24) IS documented two lines below, in the same file — this is an internal completeness gap, not a cross-file contradiction.

allFindings.title: SupprCard padding scale diverges by one tier between web and mobile despite identical prop names
allFindings.measured: Web paddingClasses: sm=p-2(8px) md=p-3(12px) lg=p-4(16px) xl=p-5(20px) [src/app/components/ui/suppr-card.tsx:67-73]. Mobile paddingValues: sm=Spacing.sm(8) md=Spacing.md(16) lg=Spacing.lg(20) xl=Spacing.xl(24) [apps/mobile/components/ui/SupprCard.tsx:105-111]. md/lg/xl are all shifted one tier apart.
allFindings.expected: Both files' docstrings claim to mirror each other ('same prop names, same variants' / 'Mirror: src/app/components/ui/suppr-card.tsx'). The same padding key should yield the same pixel value on both platforms.
allFindings.note: Concrete manifestation: TodayDashboardMacroBars passes padding="md" on both platforms (src/app/components/suppr/today-dashboard-macro-bars.tsx:137 vs apps/mobile/components/today/TodayDashboardMacroBars.tsx:143) → 12px web card padding vs 16px mobile. ProgressTrajectoryHero shows the same drift at padding="lg" (web SupprCard 16px, src/app/components/suppr/progress-hierarchy/progress-trajectory-hero.tsx:257) vs mobile's hand-rolled Spacing.lg=20px (apps/mobile/components/progress/hierarchy/ProgressTrajectoryHero.tsx:138). Call-site counts: web padding="md" ×11, "lg" ×39, "xl" ×8; mobile padding="md" ×9, "lg" ×17, "xl" ×1 — this is a systemic, not isolated, drift across dozens of cards on both platforms.

allFindings.title: Web Tailwind radius scale (--radius-sm/md/lg/xl, --radius-2xl) computes off the legal 4/6/8/12/24/full ladder
allFindings.measured: theme.css:929-932: --radius-sm: calc(var(--radius) - 4px) = 2px; --radius-md: calc(var(--radius) - 2px) = 4px; --radius-lg: var(--radius) = 6px; --radius-xl: calc(var(--radius) + 4px) = 10px (base --radius=0.375rem=6px, line 54). --radius-2xl is never redefined in this file, so `rounded-2xl` falls back to Tailwind's built-in 1rem=16px default. rounded-sm used 36×, rounded-lg 222×, rounded-xl 224×, rounded-2xl 63× across src/.
allFindings.expected: Radius rule: 'Radius snaps to: 4 / 6 / 8 / 12 / 24 / full.' 2px, 10px and 16px are all off that ladder yet are the literal computed values behind four of the most-used Tailwind radius utility classes in the web codebase.
allFindings.note: This also breaks web↔mobile parity for shared prop-name tiers even where no component bug exists: mobile Radius.lg=8/xl=12 vs web's derived rounded-lg=6/rounded-xl=10 — same tier name, different value, by token-source design rather than a call-site mistake. Concrete instance: ConfirmFoodMacroPreview tile uses web `rounded-xl` = 10px (src/app/components/food-search/ConfirmFoodMacroPreview.tsx:26) vs mobile's explicit `borderRadius: 12` (apps/mobile/components/food-search/ConfirmFoodMacroPreview.tsx:54) for the identical 3-tile P/C/F chip.

allFindings.title: TodayDashboardMacroBars row label renders as a 15px sentence-case name on web but an 11px uppercase tracked caption on mobile
allFindings.measured: Mobile: fontSize 11, fontWeight 700, letterSpacing 0.8, textTransform 'uppercase', color textSecondaryColor (dimmed) — renders 'PROTEIN' (apps/mobile/components/today/TodayDashboardMacroBars.tsx:193-204). Web: `{def.label}` — renders 'Protein' in full-strength ink, no uppercase transform, no letter-spacing (src/app/components/suppr/today-dashboard-macro-bars.tsx:155-157).
allFindings.expected: Same macro-name label (`def.label`) on the same component (Today macro-bars display mode) should render with one size/case/weight/ink treatment on both platforms.
allFindings.note: Three independent axes diverge at once (size 15→11, case sentence→upper, ink full→secondary) — this reads as two different typographic roles (a data label vs. a structural overline), not a rounding difference. Replicated across all 6 macro rows (protein/carbs/fat/fiber/sugar/sodium/water) every time either platform's user has macro-bars display selected.

allFindings.title: Digest/DigestBlended error and loading skeleton cards use Radius.lg(8px) on mobile instead of the canonical 24px card radius used everywhere else
allFindings.measured: apps/mobile/components/DigestBlended.tsx:104 and :136 (error state, loading state): `borderRadius: Radius.lg` = 8px. Same bug in the legacy twin apps/mobile/components/Digest.tsx:231 and :263. Both files' own LOADED state correctly uses `CARD_RADIUS`/`Radius.card`=24px (DigestBlended.tsx main-state block; Digest.tsx:293).
allFindings.expected: Web's error/loading/loaded states all use `rounded-card` (24px) uniformly (src/app/components/suppr/digest-blended.tsx:96, 126, 204) — matching mobile's own loaded-state value. Mobile's error/loading states should match too.
allFindings.note: Visible on every cold load and every offline/error moment of the Today/Progress week-digest card — the corner radius visibly pops from 8px to 24px once data arrives. Also both error/loading blocks use `marginBottom: 14`, a literal off the legal 4/8/12/16/20/24/32/40 spacing scale (DigestBlended.tsx:108,140,212; Digest.tsx:235,267).

allFindings.title: FoodSearchPanel confirm/log preview renders as a flat, unbordered region on web but as a bordered, success-tinted card on mobile
allFindings.measured: apps/mobile/components/food-search/FoodSearchPanel.tsx:1692-1696: `backgroundColor: colors.card, borderRadius: Radius.lg (8px), borderWidth: 1, borderColor: Accent.successSoftStrong` wraps the entire portion-picker + nutrition preview.
allFindings.expected: Web's equivalent preview (src/app/components/food-search/FoodSearchPanel.tsx:1886-1888, `<div className="flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col ...">`) has no card wrapper at all — no background, no border, no radius; content sits flush on the sheet ground.
allFindings.note: This is the primary food-logging confirmation step (Log surface, hit on every food entry). The mobile-only success-green border is also semantically odd on a neutral confirm screen (no success/error state is being communicated at that point), and the 8px radius matches neither web's flat treatment nor the canonical 24px card corner.

allFindings.title: Paywall primary CTA is a rounded-rect button on web but a fully-rounded pill on mobile, with a different type size
allFindings.measured: Mobile: `borderRadius: Radius.full` (9999px pill), label fontSize 16 (apps/mobile/components/paywall/PaywallCta.tsx:69,78). Web: `className="w-full py-3 rounded-xl bg-primary ... text-[13px] font-bold ... shadow-sm"` — rounded-xl ≈10px rect, 13px label, with a drop shadow (src/app/components/suppr/upgrade-paywall-dialog.tsx:548-551).
allFindings.expected: The single highest-value conversion CTA in the product should share one silhouette (pill vs. rect is a first-order shape difference, not a token nuance) and one type size across platforms.
allFindings.note: Paywall is a conversion surface (not one of the 6 core daily-loop surfaces), hence P1 rather than P0, but the revenue stakes make this worth prioritizing regardless.

allFindings.title: ConfirmFoodMacroPreview tile value and kcal-line text sizes are off by 1px between platforms
allFindings.measured: Mobile: tileValue fontSize 16 (apps/mobile/components/food-search/ConfirmFoodMacroPreview.tsx:62); kcalLine fontSize 14 (line 64). Web: tile value `text-base` = 15px (src/app/components/food-search/ConfirmFoodMacroPreview.tsx:32); kcal-line wrapper `text-sm` = 13px (line 37).
allFindings.expected: Identical shared component (P/C/F tile row + kcal line) should render the same numeral sizes on both platforms.
allFindings.note: Visually near-identical (1-2px), carve-out per severity rubric, but worth folding into the same pass as the tile-radius fix above (both live in the same two files).

allFindings.title: Mobile SupprCard's explicit radius="lg" override resolves to 8px, contradicting its own doc comment that it mirrors the 24px CARD_RADIUS
allFindings.measured: radiusValues.lg = Radius.lg (8px) at apps/mobile/components/ui/SupprCard.tsx:116, only consulted when a caller explicitly passes `radius="lg"`. No mobile call site currently passes an explicit `radius="lg"`/`radius="xl"` prop (grep across apps/mobile/components and apps/mobile/app found zero JSX usages — only comments referencing 'Web parity: radius="lg" (24px)' in library.tsx:72 and discover.tsx:68).
allFindings.expected: Web's equivalent prop is used at 50+ call sites (Settings.tsx, ProgressDashboard.tsx, DiscoverFeed.tsx, RecipeDetail.tsx, etc.) and always resolves to 24px per its own docstring ('lg (default — matches var(--radius-card-lg) 24px ... mirrors mobile CARD_RADIUS/TILE_RADIUS = 24)', src/app/components/ui/suppr-card.tsx:37-39). Mobile's map should resolve `radius="lg"` to 24 to match.
allFindings.note: Currently dormant (no live call site triggers it) but a landmine: any future engineer porting a web `radius="lg"` override to mobile, expecting parity per both files' docstrings, will silently ship an 8px corner instead of 24px.

allFindings.title: Digest/DigestBlended error+loading skeleton margins use an off-scale 14px literal on mobile
allFindings.measured: `marginBottom: 14` at apps/mobile/components/DigestBlended.tsx:108, 140, 212 and apps/mobile/components/Digest.tsx:235, 267 (5 instances).
allFindings.expected: Spacing rule: 'Spacing snaps to the scale: 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 20 / 24 / 32 / 40.' 14 is not on that ladder (nearest legal values are 12 or 16).
allFindings.note: Visually near-identical to 12/16px; bundling with the radius fix on the same lines (finding #4) would be the efficient remediation path.
